head: 9e21dce7cc59f22da31321b70ac6a6bfb2bf9e57

meaning: Bybit is overall available in Kazakhstan.
AVAILABLE_WITH_LIMITS is NOT presented as restricted or unavailable.
Ranking, CTA and promo eligibility remain separate decisions.
ownerAvailabilityDecision.secondaryLimitationConcept: Some eligibility and service-routing limits apply
requiredVisibleLimitationAreas: bybit.kz is the confirmed local route for core trading and KZT rails, but some bybit.com / card / referral routing remains partially resolved
Kazakhstan promotional eligibility is narrower than platform availability
local promotional amounts exist, but they must not be confused with a globally advertised maximum

presentationSemantics.note: Canonical availability→presentation mapping. AVAILABLE_WITH_LIMITS primary color is GREEN (never amber); amber is only the SECONDARY limitation indicator.

AVAILABLE_WITH_LIMITS.label: Available
AVAILABLE_WITH_LIMITS.rankingConsiderable: may be considered for ranking when the limitation does not block the user's core use case

invariants: Do not use amber as the primary availability color for AVAILABLE_WITH_LIMITS.
Bybit x Kazakhstan primary color = GREEN; secondary limitation indicator = AMBER.

rankingCtaPromoSeparation.rule: AVAILABLE_WITH_LIMITS must not automatically activate a ranking, CTA or promo. Future ranking review may consider Bybit because registration and core product access are supported, but ranking activation remains a separate owner-gated task.

correctionRequiredClaims.approvedCorrectionIntent: The Bybit Limited entity finding applies to the core local trading platform and must not automatically be applied to every ancillary bybit.kz program.
The resident-only wording cannot be presented as the complete current rule without the newer foreign-national announcement and an explicit conflict note.
Referral-code entry exists, but compatibility of a global referral code with Bybit Kazakhstan remains unverified.
Replace the old claim that no Kazakhstan-specific amount was verified. Current reviewed evidence reports two SEPARATE local programs.
handlingRules: These amounts represent different local programs.
Do not present them as one bonus.
Do not infer that the global advertised maximum applies locally.
Do not authorize a promo or CTA.

ownerInterpretation.promotions: local programs exist but eligibility is campaign-specific

importPreparationDesign.note: Design only. No transform, normalized record, canonical claim record, or MI cell was created. This is a deterministic plan for a future, separately owner-gated import task.

fieldMappings.note: source-truth review already emits captureType; validate ∈ {HTML,PDF,SCREENSHOT,API,DATASET,MANUAL_BROWSER,NEWS}
only if captureType absent: map sourceType (official_*) → HTML; discard original sourceType field
fieldMappings.default: null (require manual review, do not fabricate)

fieldMappings.rule: Use source-truth review currentStatus to determine status. Do NOT map REVIEWED blindly to ACTIVE.
fieldMappings.reject: currentStatus missing or not in enum

fieldMappings.rule: use verified official page metadata only; otherwise null

fieldMappings.relocateTo: separate claim-source linkage plan (not a market-source field)

fieldMappings.rule: preserve in QA/provenance records; do not silently discard
preserve in QA/provenance records; do not silently discard
require controlled mapping decision (single vs multi-value taxonomy); do not fabricate
decide per-source import vs derive at claim-link time; do not fabricate

transformSteps.provenancePreservation: confidence, limitations, verificationState, original sourceType retained in a QA/provenance sidecar keyed by sourceId.
transformSteps.defaultBehavior: Unknown/absent optional field → null; never invent a value.
transformSteps.rejectionCondition: Any required target field (sourceId,url,sourceTier,publisher,retrievedDate,status) unresolved → reject that record for manual review; never force ACTIVE.

transformSteps.target: normalized claim-review records (no committed schema — STRUCTURAL CONTRACT; NOT a canonical MI-cell claim)

fieldMappings.reject: any id not resolvable to a normalized market-source record

fieldMappings.rule: For the 4 correction-required claims, apply the approved correctionIntent from this decision record; carry the corrected text, never the superseded original, into any public projection.

transformSteps.correctionApplication: clm-kz-bybit-entity-003 / registration-002 / offer-001 / offer-002 corrected per this record; offer-002 keeps 1,032 USDT and 2,500 USDT as TWO separate programs.
transformSteps.unknownPreservation: Any claim/finding that remains UNKNOWN/UNVERIFIED stays UNKNOWN/UNVERIFIED; never upgraded by import.
transformSteps.rejectionCondition: verificationState CONTRADICTED without an applied correction → reject for manual review.

transformSteps.target: normalized conflict records (no committed schema — STRUCTURAL CONTRACT)

fieldMappings.rule: preserve per-service separation; never collapse into overall status

transformSteps.conflictPreservation: Both conflicts imported as PARTIALLY_RESOLVED; never auto-resolved toward a commercially convenient answer; production route unchanged.
transformSteps.rejectionCondition: status not in enum, or a resolution asserted without evidence → reject.

transformSteps.target: future exchange-market-cell CANDIDATE (schemas/market-intelligence/exchange-market-cell.schema.json) — designed only, NOT created
transformSteps.rule: Projected only after a separate owner-gated import task; keeps product statuses separate (see miCellCandidatePlan).
transformSteps.rejectionCondition: Any product status derived from availability rather than its own claim → reject.

controlledDecisionsStillRequired: productScope taxonomy (single value vs list such as ['registration','kyc','fiat_deposit'])
whether claimType is imported per-source or derived at claim-link time
updatedDate source policy (verified official page metadata only; else null)
how to represent domain/entity-routing ambiguity when one domain hosts multiple legal-program stacks

sourceNormalizationRules.sourceTypeToCaptureType: sourceType → captureType (official_* → HTML); discard original sourceType
sourceNormalizationRules.statusRule: Do NOT map REVIEWED blindly to ACTIVE. Use source-truth review currentStatus to determine ACTIVE / MOVED / REMOVED / SUPERSEDED / BLOCKED / STALE.
sourceNormalizationRules.supportedClaimIds: remove from market-source records; preserve in a separate claim-source linkage plan
sourceNormalizationRules.confidenceAndLimitations: preserve in QA/provenance records; do not silently discard
sourceNormalizationRules.productScopeAndClaimType: require controlled mapping decisions; do not fabricate
sourceNormalizationRules.updatedDate: use verified official page metadata only; otherwise null

miCellCandidatePlan.note: Future candidate field design only. Do not create the record.
miCellCandidatePlan.invariant: Do not flatten every product into the overall status; each product status derives from its own claim/finding, not from overallAvailability.

recommendationScope: Owner decision recorded + deterministic import transform designed (not executed). Import, normalized-record creation, MI-cell creation, ranking/CTA/promo, legacy-passport update, and MIGRATION_5 remain BLOCKED and separately owner-gated. Review package remains RECOVERED / UNVERIFIED.
nextTask: CBW-KZ-BYBIT-P0A-IMPORT-EXECUTE-001 — owner-gated execution of the deterministic transform designed here: build normalized market-source records + claim-source linkage + QA/provenance sidecar + conflict records, resolve the four controlled decisions (productScope, claimType, updatedDate, entity-routing), then assemble the exchange-market-cell CANDIDATE (still not production). Ranking/CTA/promo activation and MIGRATION_5 remain separate later owner-gated tasks.
